title: mentha aquatica subsp. hirsuta
intro: Mentha aquatica subsp. hirsuta, also known as hairy water mint, is a subspecies of the water mint plant that is characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The health benefits of hairy water mint include relief from nausea, headaches, and fever, as well as its ability to improve digestion and reduce stress. Hairy water mint can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine and natural health practices.
